# `lang.fsharp.core` — F#

Auto-generated. Back to [summary](../summary.md).

- **Language:** [F#](../by-language/fsharp.md)

- **Capability cells:** 3

## Capabilities

| Capability | Status | Verified at | Issue | Cites | Notes |
|------------|--------|-------------|-------|-------|-------|
| Call line precision | ✅ `full` | `2026-06-12` | 4939 | `internal/extractors/fsharp/extractor.go`<br>`internal/extractors/fsharp/fsharp_test.go`<br>`internal/resolve/dynamic_patterns_fsharp.go` | #4939 (follow-up #4906): collectCalls scans each let/member body for CALLS edges from FOUR F# call shapes — paren application `name(` / `Module.fn(` (callRE), the pipe operator `|> name` / `|> Module.fn` (pipeCallRE), the compose operator `>> fn` (composeCallRE), and (NEW #4939) SPACE-APPLIED curried application `head arg1 arg2` (spaceAppRE) — F#'s dominant call idiom (`createUser "ada"`, `json user next ctx`) that the paren/pipe/compose scanners miss. The space scanner is GATED to call positions (head must follow a clause-starter: line start / `=` / `(` / `[` / `|>` / `<|` / `->` / `;` / `,` / return / yield / do / then / else) and be followed by an argument starter (string/char/number literal, `(`/`[`, or a lower-case identifier — upper-case followers excluded as likely DU-cases/types) so it does not fire on prose, type annotations, or record fields; a literal-preserving scrub (scrubKeepingQuote keeps each literal's opening quote visible while blanking its body) lets a string argument register as an argument-starter. stripStringsAndComments still scrubs string literals (single / verbatim `@""` / triple-quoted) and `//` line + `(* *)` block comments so call-shaped tokens inside them never leak; the fsharpKeywords denylist drops control-flow / binding keywords (if/match/fun/let/...); single-letter (type-param) targets and self-recursion are filtered; targets de-duplicated per body. EVERY CALLS edge now carries a 1-based body-relative `line` Property (#4939) — matching the Nim/Erlang call_line_precision convention — computed from the call-site capture-group offset (FindAllStringSubmatchIndex). CALLS-target QUALITY is sharpened by internal/resolve/dynamic_patterns_fsharp.go (F#-unique stdlib module-qualified identifiers List./Seq./Array./Option./Result./Async./Task.* + Giraffe combinators route/routef/choose/setStatusCode/text/json + fsharp-gated common-name set printfn/sprintf/failwith/ignore/id/fst/snd). Proven by TestFSharp_SpaceApplicationCalls / _SpaceApplicationGating / _CallLineStamping (+ existing _CallsEdges / _PipeOperatorCalls / _SelfRecursionExcluded). Honest residual: the line is body-relative (offset from the operation body start), not file-absolute — file-absolute line stamping (StartLine + body line) is a documented follow-up; the space scanner is a regex head-symbol heuristic (no type/arity resolution), so partial application vs full application is not distinguished. |
| Core extraction | ✅ `full` | `2026-06-12` | 4942 | `internal/extractors/fsharp/du_record_members.go`<br>`internal/extractors/fsharp/extractor.go`<br>`internal/extractors/fsharp/fsharp_test.go` | #4906: regex-based, indentation-aware extractor (no tree-sitter F# grammar in smacker/go-tree-sitter; mirrors the Nim/Crystal precedent). moduleRE (`module [rec] Foo.Bar`) and namespaceRE emit SCOPE.Component (subtype module / namespace). letRE emits `let`/`let rec`/`let mutable` bindings as SCOPE.Operation (subtype let; signature via buildLetSig, generic `<'T>` params tolerated); memberRE emits member/override/abstract member/default definitions as SCOPE.Operation (subtype member), de-duplicated against same-name let bindings to avoid double-counting. Each operation's body is bounded by extractIndentBody (off-side-rule run of lines indented more than the declaration) and its StartLine/EndLine stamped. typeRE emits type declarations as SCOPE.Component, with classifyTypeSubtype distinguishing record / discriminated_union / interface / class / struct / alias (`= {` -> record; `= |` / body-leading `|` -> discriminated_union — the F# enum analog; interface/class/struct keywords; #4942: a single-line non-structured body `type Foo = Bar` now classifies as the distinct `alias` subtype via isAliasBody instead of the catch-all `type`). A type CONTAINS its more-indented members (memberRef CONTAINS edges via BuildOperationStructuralRef). #4942 (follow-up #4906): DU CASES and record FIELDS are now emitted as their own SCOPE.Schema sub-entities (du_record_members.go, mirroring the Rust struct-field / enum-variant precedent). extractTypeMembers parses a record/DU body — parseRecordFields splits `Name: Type` fields (multi-line and single-line `{ X:int; Y:int }`, `mutable` qualifier tolerated); parseDUCases splits `| Case [of Payload]` cases (leading-bar-optional first case, `of`-payload type captured, `with`-augmentation trimmed) — emitting one SCOPE.Schema entity per member with dotted Name `<Type>.<member>`, Subtype `field`/`du_case`, member_name/member_type/parent_class Properties, and a type→member CONTAINS edge via BuildSchemaFieldStructuralRef so the sub-entities are never orphans. isFieldName/isDUCaseName gate plausible identifiers (DU cases upper-case by F# rule). All entities/relationships tagged language=fsharp (TagEntitiesLanguage/TagRelationshipsLanguage). Proven by the fsharp_test.go suite (incl. #4942 TestFSharp_RecordFields / _DUCases / _SingleLineRecord / _TypeAlias). Deferred to follow-ups (#4942 body): computation-expression (`async{}`/`task{}`/custom builders) + active-pattern modelling, and Fable+Elmish/Feliz frontend, Serilog/MEL/printfn log_extraction, Validus/FsToolkit/DataAnnotations validation. |
| Import resolution quality | 🟢 `partial` | `2026-06-12` | 4906 | `internal/extractors/fsharp/extractor.go`<br>`internal/extractors/fsharp/fsharp_test.go`<br>`internal/substrate/fsharp.go` | #4906: collectOpenStatements parses `open Module.Path` statements (inline-comment stripped, de-duplicated) and buildImportEntities emits one IMPORTS edge per opened module to a SCOPE.Component placeholder (importDisplayName normalises `Microsoft.FSharp.Collections` -> `Collections`); every module/type/operation entity also carries the file's open-set on Properties["imports"]. The constant-binding sniffer internal/substrate/fsharp.go additionally recognises module-level `[<Literal>] let X = "..."` and `Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable(...) |> Option.defaultValue` / `defaultArg` env-default shapes plus `open` paths. Partial (honest): IMPORTS edges target the raw opened-module string, not a resolved module entity, and F# `open`-shadowing / auto-open / type-extension resolution is not modelled. Symbol-level resolution is a follow-up. |
